title: Free-shipping threshold $35 vs $50
hypothesis: Lowering the free-shipping threshold lifts conversion enough to offset margin.
learning: Won on conversion but the AOV guardrail nearly tripped; finance review required before similar threshold tests.

title: $0-deposit checkout for service bookings
hypothesis: Removing the upfront deposit reduces abandonment.
learning: Largest single win of the year; cancellation rate rose 1.1pt but net bookings strongly positive.

title: Urgency badge on PDP (low stock)
hypothesis: Scarcity messaging lifts add-to-cart.
result: +3.8% ATC initially; effect decayed to +0.6% by week 4
learning: Classic novelty effect — re-read long-running tests at 4+ weeks before declaring durable wins.

title: Checkout form: 11 fields to 6
hypothesis: Fewer fields, higher completion.
learning: Field removal beat field reordering tested in EXP-018; address autocomplete drove most of the gain.

title: Review snippets in search results
hypothesis: Social proof in SERP lifts CTR to PDP.
learning: Upstream CTR gains don't automatically convert; instrument the full funnel before celebrating.

title: Price anchoring: was/now display
hypothesis: Reference pricing lifts conversion on discounted items.
learning: Segment-scoped ship: full-price items showed nothing; targeting beats blanket rollout.

title: Exit-intent email capture modal
hypothesis: Capturing emails at exit grows the remarketing pool.
learning: The modal cannibalized active sessions; list growth is not worth same-session revenue loss.

title: Cart-abandon reminder at 1h vs 24h
hypothesis: Earlier reminders recover more carts.
learning: Recency beat discount depth (EXP-029); timing is the cheapest lever in lifecycle.

title: Onboarding checklist for new accounts
hypothesis: A 3-step checklist lifts week-1 activation.
learning: Activation lifted but didn't compound into retention; pair checklists with a habit hook next time.

title: Search autocomplete with category suggestions
hypothesis: Guided queries reduce null results.
learning: Directionally positive but marginal at the planned sample; extended two weeks rather than calling early.

title: Installer-availability shown before quote
hypothesis: Showing real capacity earlier reduces drop-off at scheduling.
learning: Setting expectations early beat optimizing the scheduling screen itself (EXP-045).

title: Loyalty banner on homepage
hypothesis: Promoting the loyalty program lifts signups.
learning: Bot traffic skewed assignment; the SRM check saved a false win. Re-run came back at +6% real.

title: Delivery-promise date on PDP
hypothesis: A concrete arrival date lifts add-to-cart.
learning: Specificity beats speed claims; 'Get it by Tue' outperformed 'Fast shipping' (EXP-049).

title: Personalized homepage modules
hypothesis: Behavioral personalization lifts engagement and conversion.
learning: Cold-start users saw no lift; gains concentrated in returning users with 3+ sessions.

title: One-step vs two-step lead form
hypothesis: Splitting the form into two steps lifts completion.
learning: Progressive disclosure won; monitor sales-accepted rate as the quality guardrail.

title: AI-ranked lead routing
hypothesis: Routing by model score lifts qualified-lead rate vs round-robin.
learning: Score-based routing held up under a 4-week holdout; retrain cadence set to monthly.

title: BNPL option at checkout
hypothesis: Buy-now-pay-later lifts conversion on carts over $200.
result: +4.4% conversion on qualifying carts (p=0.004), refund rate flat
learning: Gains concentrated in $200-$600 carts; no effect below the threshold.

title: Promo code field hidden behind link
hypothesis: Hiding the promo field reduces code-hunting abandonment.
learning: Open field invited exits to coupon sites; margin improved alongside conversion.

title: Search filters: left rail vs top bar
hypothesis: Top-bar filters increase filter usage on mobile.
learning: Engagement without conversion is rearranged furniture; needed a stronger downstream link.

title: Recommendation carousel on cart page
hypothesis: Cross-sell on cart lifts AOV without hurting completion.
learning: Cart-page recs are net positive only when capped at one row; two rows tested worse in EXP-052.

title: Guest checkout default
hypothesis: Defaulting to guest checkout lifts completion vs account-first.
learning: Post-purchase account prompt recovered half the lost signups; sequence beats gating.

title: Push notification timing: morning vs evening
hypothesis: Evening pushes drive higher session starts.
learning: Opens bought with attention have an uninstall tax; caps made it net positive.

title: Referral incentive: give $10 vs give $20
hypothesis: Doubling the incentive doubles referral volume.
result: +38% referrals at $20, CAC payback lengthened 2.1 months
learning: Volume responded but unit economics marginal; extended to read LTV of referred cohort.

title: Review request email at 7 vs 21 days
hypothesis: Earlier review requests lift review volume.
learning: Early reviewers skew critical for installed products; routed services SKUs to 14 days.

title: Shipping cost shown on PDP vs at checkout
hypothesis: Early shipping transparency reduces checkout abandonment.
result: Checkout abandonment -3.8pt, PDP exit +1.1pt, net conversion +1.6% (p=0.03)
learning: Moves the drop-off earlier where it's cheaper; net funnel math decided it.

title: Returns policy badge near add-to-cart
hypothesis: Visible free-returns promise lifts ATC.
learning: Return-rate guardrail held under the 1pt threshold finance set after EXP-014.

title: Account creation: social sign-on added
hypothesis: OAuth options lift signup completion.
learning: Apple sign-on alone drove two-thirds of the lift; email verification drop-off was the real enemy.

title: Subscription save-offer on cancel flow
hypothesis: A pause option reduces hard cancels.
result: Hard cancels -19% (p<0.001), 41% of pauses resumed within 60 days
learning: Pause beats discount: EXP-072's 20%-off save offer retained worse and cost margin.

title: Price-drop alerts on wishlisted items
hypothesis: Alerts convert dormant wishlists.
learning: Highest-ROI lifecycle trigger tested this year; volume capped by wishlist adoption.

title: Checkout trust badges (security icons)
hypothesis: Trust signals lift completion for new visitors.
learning: Segment-only wins from exploratory cuts need a targeted re-test, not a blanket ship — see EXP-033 for the scoped pattern.

title: Step-up verification on high-risk orders
hypothesis: Targeted friction cuts fraud without hurting good users.
learning: Risk-scored friction beat blanket friction (EXP-066) on both fraud and conversion.

title: Velocity limits on promo redemption
hypothesis: Rate-limiting code redemptions curbs abuse rings.
result: Abusive redemptions -64%, legitimate redemption friction complaints +12/wk
learning: Power-user allowlist resolved most complaints; abuse migrated to gift cards (EXP-097 follow-up).

title: Page speed: defer third-party scripts
hypothesis: Faster LCP lifts conversion.
learning: Performance is a feature; effect strongest on mid-tier Android devices.

title: Sticky add-to-cart bar on mobile PDP
hypothesis: Persistent CTA lifts mobile ATC.
learning: Mobile-only win; desktop variant tested flat and added clutter.

title: Seasonal homepage takeover
hypothesis: Holiday hero treatment lifts engagement and revenue.
learning: Attention shifted between modules without creating net revenue; takeovers need exclusive inventory.

title: Live chat proactive trigger at 60s on checkout
hypothesis: Proactive chat rescues stuck checkouts.
result: +2.1% completion among triggered users (p=0.03), support cost +9%
learning: Unit economics only clear above the cart-value floor; scoping rule came from the finance model.

title: Address autocomplete provider swap
hypothesis: Faster autocomplete reduces address-step abandonment.
learning: Vendor latency was the hidden variable; instrument third-party SLAs inside funnel analytics.

title: Quantity discounts surfaced on PDP
hypothesis: Visible bulk pricing lifts units per order.
learning: Worked only on consumables; durable goods showed nothing — scope by replenishment category.

title: Email subject: question vs statement framing
hypothesis: Question framing lifts opens.
learning: Opens are a vanity numerator when clicks don't follow; moved testing to body CTA.

title: Saved-cart sync across devices
hypothesis: Cross-device carts lift multi-session conversion.
learning: Identity resolution was the prerequisite investment; the feature was the easy part.
